task_yaml.task:
I'm presenting on Mistral 7B (arxiv 2310.06825) at a reading group
next week and want to draft a poster panel — produce a PDF first, then
I'll refine the layout myself later.

The panel should look roughly like this: top section has the paper
title, first few authors, and page count; middle section has 5 core
concepts, each with a short Wikipedia blurb (~80 characters from the
first paragraph), annotated with whether that concept appears in the
paper — ✓ if found, ✗ if not. Overall it should look like a poster
panel, not a wall of text.

I've pinned 5 specific Wikipedia revisions (to avoid future edits
breaking things) — please use these exact oldids:
  1. Mistral_AI                            oldid=1352105656
  2. Large_language_model                  oldid=1351510195
  3. Mixture_of_experts                    oldid=1346279093
  4. Generative_pre-trained_transformer    oldid=1351783271
  5. Open-source_artificial_intelligence   oldid=1350744847

Download the paper PDF with wget, convert to text with pdftotext, then
search for keywords to determine concept presence — e.g. "mixture of
experts", "large language model", "open-source" — finding any match
counts as ✓. Extract paper metadata with pdfinfo. Fetch Wikipedia data
with curl via the MediaWiki API (pass the revids parameter to lock the
version).

Data deliverables: paper metadata (arxiv_id, title, full authors list
as authors, page_count) saved as paper_meta.json; the 5 concepts' Wiki
results saved as wiki_lookups.json with a concepts array in the 1-5
order above, each containing name, wiki_revision_id, wiki_url, and
first_para_first_100_chars; cross-check results saved as
cross_check.csv with header: concept,paper_mentions,wiki_revision_id,
verification_status — mark those found in the paper as
verified_in_paper, those not found as wiki_only; multi-language
availability of the 5 concepts on Chinese/Japanese Wikipedia saved as
multi_lang_availability.json, also using a concepts array with each
entry containing name, en, zh, ja — values are "yes" or "no" (check
via the langlinks API).

Build the poster panel with inkscape — source file poster.svg goes in
/tmp_workspace/work/ for later refinement, then export a
poster_panel.pdf from the SVG.

Finally open poster_panel.pdf with evince (run headless under xvfb)
and take a screenshot named evince_screenshot.png so I can preview the
rendering.

Except for poster.svg in /tmp_workspace/work/, put all other artifacts
under /tmp_workspace/results/. When done, just tell me which concepts
got ✓ and which got ✗.
